mentions.note: Caller Jim asks about Kraft (merged 2011); notes Warren Buffett divesting 28% stake; Cramer says new CEO Steve Carell came from Kellogg is okay, but he and Ben Stole can't get behind it—not enough good happening in food business; Cramer is a seller, not a buyer.
No growth despite CEO efforts; avoiding no-growth + yield combo; won't outperform despite good yield; looking for growth opportunities instead.
Down considerably; packaged food inflation indicator.
New CEO Cahillane executing Win/Hold/Win Big portfolio strategy; investing behind brands with competitive advantage; capacity utilization jumped 29%→54%.

Faith-based conviction in CEO Steve Caple's turnaround based on Kellogg track record; 6.85% dividend yield. Previous management gutted company but Caple's vision could 'turbocharge old lost brands'—requires belief in execution.
